module.title: ABC Electronics India - SAP TM End-to-End Implementation

topic.title: Lab 5: Truck Breakdown During Transit & Transportation Replanning

learningObjectives.title: Manage in-transit transportation exceptions
learningObjectives.description: Handle truck breakdown events using structured impact analysis and replanning.

learningObjectives.title: Evaluate business and cost trade-offs
learningObjectives.description: Compare repair wait time versus replacement resource assignment against SLA commitments.

learningObjectives.title: Coordinate cross-functional incident response
learningObjectives.description: Align planner, carrier, customer service and management decisions during disruptions.

learningObjectives.title: Update execution plans and communication
learningObjectives.description: Record exceptions, revise ETA and continue shipment execution with approved decisions.

sections.title: Real-Time Lab 5: Truck Breakdown During Transit & Transportation Replanning

sections.content: The shipment successfully left the Bengaluru Distribution Center after the warehouse delay was resolved in Lab 4. Two hours into the journey, the carrier's driver reports that the truck has broken down on NH-48 near Krishnagiri due to a gearbox failure. The shipment contains high-value laptops worth INR 2.5 Crores and must still reach the customer before 9:00 AM tomorrow.

sections.title: Your Role
sections.content: You are the SAP TM Production Support Consultant on duty. The transportation planner, carrier manager, customer service team and warehouse manager are waiting for your recommendation.

Waiting six hours for repairs will almost certainly cause the customer to miss the agreed delivery time. A replacement truck is available 40 kilometers away, but transferring the cargo will increase transportation costs by INR 18,000.

sections.title: Business Opinions
rows: Customer must receive shipment before SLA expires.
Repair truck instead of replacing it.
On-time delivery is more important than cost.

sections.title: Decision Options
rows: Lowest cost but SLA violation is highly likely.
Higher transportation cost but delivery commitment maintained.
Major customer dissatisfaction and financial loss.

sections.content: Approve the replacement vehicle immediately. Update the Freight Order execution details, record the transportation exception, notify the customer about the revised ETA, obtain management approval for the additional transportation cost and continue shipment execution. The additional INR 18,000 transportation cost is significantly lower than the potential penalties, lost business and customer dissatisfaction caused by missing the delivery commitment.

sections.title: Consultant Best Practice
sections.content: During transportation exceptions, prioritize business continuity and customer commitments. Every replanning decision should be documented with its business justification, financial impact and stakeholder approval.

sections.title: Real Production Incident
sections.content: A consumer electronics company transporting premium smartphones experienced a truck engine failure during transit. Instead of waiting for repairs, the SAP TM planning team reassigned the shipment to another carrier within one hour. Although transportation costs increased by 12%, the company avoided SLA penalties worth over INR 8 lakhs and retained an important retail customer.

sections.title: Accenture Production Scenario
sections.content: If a truck breaks down during transportation, what is your first action? A consultant should answer: ensure safety, verify cargo condition, assess business impact, evaluate alternative resources, communicate with stakeholders and then update SAP TM execution records and transportation plans.

items: Project continues to Lab 6: Customer Changes Delivery Date During Transit.
